text:
There was once a small village surrounded by forest. Every village child knew the tale of the Whispering Wood: tales told by lantern lights at sundown. The creatures of the wood were friendly once seen—until dark.

In a small corner of the forest grew an ancient oak. Beneath its broad boughs was a nest. This wasn’t just any nest—it was home to a family of owlets. Each day, as twilight draped the world in shades of twilight blue, the owlets would hatch, practice their cry, and learn about the dangers and wonders of their woodland hideaway. They were as curious as a cat about everything.

One particular owl, Winston, was unusually quiet. He had listened to many stories but never believed the forest was inhabited by creatures that were anything but birds. One moonlit night, while his siblings hooted softly, Winston followed a faint sound deeper into the wood. It was a melody unlike any owl song—a tune that seemed to hum quietly by
